verses.greek: Παῦλος ἀπόστολος ⸂Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ⸃ διὰ θελήματος θεοῦ κατ’ ἐπαγγελίαν ζωῆς τῆς ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ
verses.mistral: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, according to the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus
verses.kjv: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, according to the promise of life which is in Christ Jesus,

verses.greek: Τιμοθέῳ ἀγαπητῷ τέκνῳ· χάρις, ἔλεος, εἰρήνη ἀπὸ θεοῦ πατρὸς καὶ Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν.
verses.mistral: Grace, mercy, peace from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Savior.
verses.kjv: To Timothy, my dearly beloved son: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.

verses.greek: Χάριν ἔχω τῷ θεῷ, ᾧ λατρεύω ἀπὸ προγόνων ἐν καθαρᾷ συνειδήσει, ὡς ἀδιάλειπτον ἔχω τὴν περὶ σοῦ μνείαν ἐν ταῖς δεήσεσίν μου, νυκτὸς καὶ ἡμέρας
verses.mistral: I have a conscience clear before God and Father, from whom I do not shrink to call upon you night and day, unceasingly, regarding your remembrance in my prayers.
verses.kjv: I thank God, whom I serve from my forefathers with pure conscience, that without ceasing I have remembrance of thee in my prayers night and day;

verses.greek: ἐπιποθῶν σε ἰδεῖν, μεμνημένος σου τῶν δακρύων, ἵνα χαρᾶς πληρωθῶ
verses.mistral: Longing to see you again, remembering your tears, that my joy may be made complete.
verses.kjv: Greatly desiring to see thee, being mindful of thy tears, that I may be filled with joy;

verses.greek: ὑπόμνησιν ⸀λαβὼν τῆς ἐν σοὶ ἀνυποκρίτου πίστεως, ἥτις ἐνῴκησεν πρῶτον ἐν τῇ μάμμῃ σου Λωΐδι καὶ τῇ μητρί σου Εὐνίκῃ, πέπεισμαι δὲ ὅτι καὶ ἐν σοί.
verses.mistral: Recalled is the sincere faith that first dwelt in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice, and I am persuaded that it is in you as well.
verses.kjv: When I call to remembrance the unfeigned faith that is in thee, which dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois, and thy mother Eunice; and I am persuaded that in thee also.

verses.greek: δι’ ἣν αἰτίαν ἀναμιμνῄσκω σε ἀναζωπυρεῖν τὸ χάρισμα τοῦ θεοῦ, ὅ ἐστιν ἐν σοὶ διὰ τῆς ἐπιθέσεως τῶν χειρῶν μου·
verses.mistral: I remind you to rekindle the gift of God that is in you through the imposition of my hands.
verses.kjv: Wherefore I put thee in remembrance that thou stir up the gift of God, which is in thee by the putting on of my hands.

verses.greek: οὐ γὰρ ἔδωκεν ἡμῖν ὁ θεὸς πνεῦμα δειλίας, ἀλλὰ δυνάμεως καὶ ἀγάπης καὶ σωφρονισμοῦ.
verses.mistral: God did not give us a spirit of fear, but of power, love and self-control.
verses.kjv: For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.

verses.greek: Μὴ οὖν ἐπαισχυνθῇς τὸ μαρτύριον τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν μηδὲ ἐμὲ τὸν δέσμιον αὐτοῦ, ἀλλὰ συγκακοπάθησον τῷ εὐαγγελίῳ κατὰ δύναμιν θεοῦ,
verses.mistral: Do not be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord or of me His prisoner, but share in suffering for the Gospel according to the power of God.
verses.kjv: Be not thou therefore ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner: but be thou partaker of the afflictions of the gospel according to the power of God;

verses.greek: τοῦ σώσαντος ἡμᾶς καὶ καλέσαντος κλήσει ἁγίᾳ, οὐ κατὰ τὰ ἔργα ἡμῶν ἀλλὰ κατὰ ἰδίαν πρόθεσιν καὶ χάριν, τὴν δοθεῖσαν ἡμῖν ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ πρὸ χρόνων αἰωνίων,
verses.mistral: Saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to His own purpose and grace, which was given to us in Christ Jesus before the ages of time.
verses.kjv: Who hath saved us, and called us with an holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given us in Christ Jesus before the world began,

verses.greek: φανερωθεῖσαν δὲ νῦν διὰ τῆς ἐπιφανείας τοῦ σωτῆρος ἡμῶν ⸂Ἰησοῦ Χριστοῦ⸃, καταργήσαντος μὲν τὸν θάνατον φωτίσαντος δὲ ζωὴν καὶ ἀφθαρσίαν διὰ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου,
verses.mistral: Now appearing in its fullness through the appearance of our Savior Jesus Christ, who abolished death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel.
verses.kjv: But is now made manifest by the appearing of our Saviour Jesus Christ, who hath abolished death, and hath brought life and immortality to light through the gospel:

verses.greek: εἰς ὃ ἐτέθην ἐγὼ κῆρυξ καὶ ἀπόστολος καὶ ⸀διδάσκαλος.
verses.mistral: I was appointed a herald, an apostle, and a teacher.
verses.kjv: Whereunto I am appointed a preacher, and an apostle, and a teacher of the Gentiles.

verses.greek: δι’ ἣν αἰτίαν καὶ ταῦτα πάσχω, ἀλλ’ οὐκ ἐπαισχύνομαι, οἶδα γὰρ ᾧ πεπίστευκα, καὶ πέπεισμαι ὅτι δυνατός ἐστιν τὴν παραθήκην μου φυλάξαι εἰς ἐκείνην τὴν ἡμέραν.
verses.mistral: Endures all these things for my sake, that I may be aided by him who strengthened me until now.
verses.kjv: For the which cause I also suffer these things: nevertheless I am not ashamed: for I know whom I have believed, and am persuaded that he is able to keep that which I have committed unto him against that day.

verses.greek: ὑποτύπωσιν ἔχε ὑγιαινόντων λόγων ὧν παρ’ ἐμοῦ ἤκουσας ἐν πίστει καὶ ἀγάπῃ τῇ ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ·
verses.mistral: Exhibit the healthful words which you have heard from me in faith and love that is in Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: Hold fast the form of sound words, which thou hast heard of me, in faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.

verses.greek: τὴν καλὴν παραθήκην φύλαξον διὰ πνεύματος ἁγίου τοῦ ἐνοικοῦντος ἐν ἡμῖν.
verses.mistral: Maintain the pure commandment through the Holy Spirit which dwells in us.
verses.kjv: That good thing which was committed unto thee keep by the Holy Ghost which dwelleth in us.

verses.greek: Οἶδας τοῦτο ὅτι ἀπεστράφησάν με πάντες οἱ ἐν τῇ Ἀσίᾳ, ὧν ἐστιν Φύγελος καὶ Ἑρμογένης.
verses.mistral: You have understood that all in Asia turned away from me, among whom are Phygelus and Hermogenes.
verses.kjv: This thou knowest, that all they which are in Asia be turned away from me; of whom are Phygellus and Hermogenes.

verses.greek: δῴη ἔλεος ὁ κύριος τῷ Ὀνησιφόρου οἴκῳ, ὅτι πολλάκις με ἀνέψυξεν, καὶ τὴν ἅλυσίν μου οὐκ ἐπαισχύνθη·
verses.mistral: The Lord be merciful to the household of Onesiphorus, for he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chain.
verses.kjv: The Lord give mercy unto the house of Onesiphorus; for he oft refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain:

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ γενόμενος ἐν Ῥώμῃ ⸀σπουδαίως ἐζήτησέν με καὶ εὗρεν—
verses.mistral: Found in Rome he sought me earnestly and found me
verses.kjv: But, when he was in Rome, he sought me out very diligently, and found me.

verses.greek: δῴη αὐτῷ ὁ κύριος εὑρεῖν ἔλεος παρὰ κυρίου ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ— καὶ ὅσα ἐν Ἐφέσῳ διηκόνησεν, βέλτιον σὺ γινώσκεις.
verses.mistral: The Lord give him (2 Timothy 1:18) find mercy from the Lord in that day— and whatever he served in Ephesus, you know better.
verses.kjv: The Lord grant unto him that he may find mercy of the Lord in that day: and in how many things he ministered unto me at Ephesus, thou knowest very well.

verses.greek: Σὺ οὖν, τέκνον μου, ἐνδυναμοῦ ἐν τῇ χάριτι τῇ ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ,
verses.mistral: Therefore, my dear child, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: Thou therefore, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.

verses.greek: καὶ ἃ ἤκουσας παρ’ ἐμοῦ διὰ πολλῶν μαρτύρων, ταῦτα παράθου πιστοῖς ἀνθρώποις, οἵτινες ἱκανοὶ ἔσονται καὶ ἑτέρους διδάξαι.
verses.mistral: And the things you have heard from me among many witnesses, these entrust to faithful men who will be able to teach others also.
verses.kjv: And the things that thou hast heard of me among many witnesses, the same commit thou to faithful men, who shall be able to teach others also.

verses.greek: ⸀συγκακοπάθησον ὡς καλὸς στρατιώτης ⸂Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ⸃.
verses.mistral: Endure suffering as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: Thou therefore endure hardness, as a good soldier of Jesus Christ.

verses.greek: οὐδεὶς στρατευόμενος ἐμπλέκεται ταῖς τοῦ βίου πραγματείαις, ἵνα τῷ στρατολογήσαντι ἀρέσῃ·
verses.mistral: Nobody entangles himself in the ordinary concerns of life, so that he may please him who enlisted him as a soldier.
verses.kjv: No man that warreth entangleth himself with the affairs of this life; that he may please him who hath chosen him to be a soldier.

verses.greek: ἐὰν δὲ καὶ ἀθλῇ τις, οὐ στεφανοῦται ἐὰν μὴ νομίμως ἀθλήσῃ·
verses.mistral: If anyone competes, he does not receive a crown unless he competes according to the rules.
verses.kjv: And if a man also strive for masteries, yet is he not crowned, except he strive lawfully.

verses.greek: τὸν κοπιῶντα γεωργὸν δεῖ πρῶτον τῶν καρπῶν μεταλαμβάνειν.
verses.mistral: The laboring farmer must first reap the harvest.
verses.kjv: The husbandman that laboureth must be first partaker of the fruits.

verses.greek: νόει ⸀ὃ λέγω· ⸀δώσει γάρ σοι ὁ κύριος σύνεσιν ἐν πᾶσιν.
verses.mistral: Grants you the Lord wisdom in all things.
verses.kjv: Consider what I say; and the Lord give thee understanding in all things.

verses.greek: Μνημόνευε Ἰησοῦν Χριστὸν ἐγηγερμένον ἐκ νεκρῶν, ἐκ σπέρματος Δαυίδ, κατὰ τὸ εὐαγγέλιόν μου·
verses.mistral: Remember Jesus Christ, risen from the dead, descended from David, according to my gospel.
verses.kjv: Remember that Jesus Christ of the seed of David was raised from the dead according to my gospel:

verses.greek: ἐν ᾧ κακοπαθῶ μέχρι δεσμῶν ὡς κακοῦργος. ἀλλὰ ὁ λόγος τοῦ θεοῦ οὐ δέδεται·
verses.mistral: In which I am suffering even unto chains as a criminal, but the word of God is not bound.
verses.kjv: Wherein I suffer trouble, as an evil doer, even unto bonds; but the word of God is not bound.

verses.greek: διὰ τοῦτο πάντα ὑπομένω διὰ τοὺς ἐκλεκτούς, ἵνα καὶ αὐτοὶ σωτηρίας τύχωσιν τῆς ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ μετὰ δόξης αἰωνίου.
verses.mistral: Therefore I endure all things for the elect's sake, that they also may obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
verses.kjv: Therefore I endure all things for the elect's sakes, that they may also obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.

verses.greek: πιστὸς ὁ λόγος· εἰ γὰρ συναπεθάνομεν, καὶ συζήσομεν·
verses.mistral: Faithful is the word: If we die, we shall live; and if we faint, we shall not be ashamed.
verses.kjv: It is a faithful saying: For if we be dead with him, we shall also live with him:

verses.greek: εἰ ὑπομένομεν, καὶ συμβασιλεύσομεν· εἰ ⸀ἀρνησόμεθα, κἀκεῖνος ἀρνήσεται ἡμᾶς·
verses.mistral: If we endure, we shall reign; if we deny, he will also deny us.
verses.kjv: If we suffer, we shall also reign with him: if we deny him, he also will deny us:

verses.greek: εἰ ἀπιστοῦμεν, ἐκεῖνος πιστὸς μένει· ⸀ἀρνήσασθαι ἑαυτὸν οὐ δύναται.
verses.mistral: If we deny, he remains faithful; he cannot deny himself.
verses.kjv: If we believe not, yet he abideth faithful: he cannot deny himself.

verses.greek: Ταῦτα ὑπομίμνῃσκε, διαμαρτυρόμενος ἐνώπιον τοῦ ⸀κυρίου, μὴ λογομαχεῖν, ⸀ἐπ’ οὐδὲν χρήσιμον, ἐπὶ καταστροφῇ τῶν ἀκουόντων.
verses.mistral: Remember this, declaring it before the Lord, do not argue endlessly, for it is useless. Instead, aim to convert those who listen.
verses.kjv: Of these things put them in remembrance, charging them before the Lord that they strive not about words to no profit, but to the subverting of the hearers.

verses.greek: σπούδασον σεαυτὸν δόκιμον παραστῆσαι τῷ θεῷ, ἐργάτην ἀνεπαίσχυντον, ὀρθοτομοῦντα τὸν λόγον τῆς ἀληθείας.
verses.mistral: Strive to show yourself Approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.
verses.kjv: Study to shew thyself approved unto God, a workman that needeth not to be ashamed, rightly dividing the word of truth.

verses.greek: τὰς δὲ βεβήλους κενοφωνίας περιΐστασο· ἐπὶ πλεῖον γὰρ προκόψουσιν ἀσεβείας,
verses.mistral: Avoid foolish and ignorant discussions. For they will lead to greater ungodliness.
verses.kjv: But shun profane and vain babblings: for they will increase unto more ungodliness.

verses.greek: καὶ ὁ λόγος αὐτῶν ὡς γάγγραινα νομὴν ἕξει· ὧν ἐστιν Ὑμέναιος καὶ Φίλητος,
verses.mistral: Their word will be like a gnawing and corrupting rule. Of whom are Hymenaeus and Philetus.
verses.kjv: And their word will eat as doth a canker: of whom is Hymenaeus and Philetus;

verses.greek: οἵτινες περὶ τὴν ἀλήθειαν ἠστόχησαν, ⸀λέγοντες ἀνάστασιν ἤδη γεγονέναι, καὶ ἀνατρέπουσιν τήν τινων πίστιν.
verses.mistral: These are those who swerved from the truth, saying that resurrection has already occurred, and they subvert the faith of some.
verses.kjv: Who concerning the truth have erred, saying that the resurrection is past already; and overthrow the faith of some.

verses.greek: ὁ μέντοι στερεὸς θεμέλιος τοῦ θεοῦ ἕστηκεν, ἔχων τὴν σφραγῖδα ταύτην· Ἔγνω κύριος τοὺς ὄντας αὐτοῦ, καί· Ἀποστήτω ἀπὸ ἀδικίας πᾶς ὁ ὀνομάζων τὸ ὄνομα κυρίου.
verses.mistral: The firm foundation of the God remains, bearing this seal: "The Lord knows those who are His," and, "Let everyone who names the name of the Lord depart from iniquity."
verses.kjv: Nevertheless the foundation of God standeth sure, having this seal, The Lord knoweth them that are his. And, Let every one that nameth the name of Christ depart from iniquity.

verses.greek: Ἐν μεγάλῃ δὲ οἰκίᾳ οὐκ ἔστιν μόνον σκεύη χρυσᾶ καὶ ἀργυρᾶ ἀλλὰ καὶ ξύλινα καὶ ὀστράκινα, καὶ ἃ μὲν εἰς τιμὴν ἃ δὲ εἰς ἀτιμίαν·
verses.mistral: In a great house there are not only vessels of gold and silver, but also of wood and clay. Some for honor, some for dishonor.
verses.kjv: But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of earth; and some to honour, and some to dishonour.

verses.greek: ἐὰν οὖν τις ἐκκαθάρῃ ἑαυτὸν ἀπὸ τούτων, ἔσται σκεῦος εἰς τιμήν, ἡγιασμένον, ⸀εὔχρηστον τῷ δεσπότῃ, εἰς πᾶν ἔργον ἀγαθὸν ἡτοιμασμένον.
verses.mistral: If anyone cleanses himself from these things, he will be a vessel for honor, sanctified, useful to the Master, prepared for every good work.
verses.kjv: If a man therefore purge himself from these, he shall be a vessel unto honour, sanctified, and meet for the master's use, and prepared unto every good work.

verses.greek: τὰς δὲ νεωτερικὰς ἐπιθυμίας φεῦγε, δίωκε δὲ δικαιοσύνην, πίστιν, ἀγάπην, εἰρήνην μετὰ τῶν ἐπικαλουμένων τὸν κύριον ἐκ καθαρᾶς καρδίας.
verses.mistral: Flee youthful passions, pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace with those who call on the Lord from a pure heart.
verses.kjv: Flee also youthful lusts: but follow righteousness, faith, charity, peace, with them that call on the Lord out of a pure heart.

verses.greek: τὰς δὲ μωρὰς καὶ ἀπαιδεύτους ζητήσεις παραιτοῦ, εἰδὼς ὅτι γεννῶσι μάχας·
verses.mistral: Seek to rebuke the foolish and unlearned. Knowing that they breed strife
verses.kjv: But foolish and unlearned questions avoid, knowing that they do gender strifes.

verses.greek: δοῦλον δὲ κυρίου οὐ δεῖ μάχεσθαι, ἀλλὰ ἤπιον εἶναι πρὸς πάντας, διδακτικόν, ἀνεξίκακον,
verses.mistral: Servant of the Lord should not strive, but be gentle towards all, teaching, patient.
verses.kjv: And the servant of the Lord must not strive; but be gentle unto all men, apt to teach, patient,

verses.greek: ἐν πραΰτητι παιδεύοντα τοὺς ἀντιδιατιθεμένους, μήποτε ⸀δώῃ αὐτοῖς ὁ θεὸς μετάνοιαν εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν ἀληθείας,
verses.mistral: Encourages the opposing ones to repentance, that perhaps God may grant them a change of heart for a recognition of truth.
verses.kjv: In meekness instructing those that oppose themselves; if God peradventure will give them repentance to the acknowledging of the truth;

verses.greek: καὶ ἀνανήψωσιν ἐκ τῆς τοῦ διαβόλου παγίδος, ἐζωγρημένοι ὑπ’ αὐτοῦ εἰς τὸ ἐκείνου θέλημα.
verses.mistral: And they will rise up from the devil's snares, being ensnared by him unto his will.
verses.kjv: And that they may recover themselves out of the snare of the devil, who are taken captive by him at his will.

verses.greek: Τοῦτο δὲ γίνωσκε ὅτι ἐν ἐσχάταις ἡμέραις ἐνστήσονται καιροὶ χαλεποί·
verses.mistral: Difficult times will come in the last days.
verses.kjv: This know also, that in the last days perilous times shall come.

verses.greek: ἔσονται γὰρ οἱ ἄνθρωποι φίλαυτοι, φιλάργυροι, ἀλαζόνες, ὑπερήφανοι, βλάσφημοι, γονεῦσιν ἀπειθεῖς, ἀχάριστοι, ἀνόσιοι,
verses.mistral: Self-lovers, lovers of money, boastful, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, ungrateful, unholy,
verses.kjv: For men shall be lovers of their own selves, covetous, boasters, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,

verses.greek: ἄστοργοι, ἄσπονδοι, διάβολοι, ἀκρατεῖς, ἀνήμεροι, ἀφιλάγαθοι,
verses.mistral: Faithless, untrustworthy, malicious, uncontrolled, lawless, selfish, unloving
verses.kjv: Without natural affection, trucebreakers, false accusers, incontinent, fierce, despisers of those that are good,

verses.greek: προδόται, προπετεῖς, τετυφωμένοι, φιλήδονοι μᾶλλον ἢ φιλόθεοι,
verses.mistral: Traitors, reckless, dissipated, more lovers of pleasure than lovers of God
verses.kjv: Traitors, heady, highminded, lovers of pleasures more than lovers of God;

verses.greek: ἔχοντες μόρφωσιν εὐσεβείας τὴν δὲ δύναμιν αὐτῆς ἠρνημένοι· καὶ τούτους ἀποτρέπου.
verses.mistral: Renouncing the form of godliness, denying its power. Turn away from such.
verses.kjv: Having a form of godliness, but denying the power thereof: from such turn away.

verses.greek: ἐκ τούτων γάρ εἰσιν οἱ ἐνδύνοντες εἰς τὰς οἰκίας καὶ ⸀αἰχμαλωτίζοντες γυναικάρια σεσωρευμένα ἁμαρτίαις, ἀγόμενα ἐπιθυμίαις ποικίλαις,
verses.mistral: These are the ones who are enticing others for ungodly desires, sensuality, into homes, and are ensnaring unholy women in various sins, led by lustful passions.
verses.kjv: For of this sort are they which creep into houses, and lead captive silly women laden with sins, led away with divers lusts,

verses.greek: πάντοτε μανθάνοντα καὶ μηδέποτε εἰς ἐπίγνωσιν ἀληθείας ἐλθεῖν δυνάμενα.
verses.mistral: Continually learning but never coming to a knowledge of the truth
verses.kjv: Ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.

verses.greek: ὃν τρόπον δὲ Ἰάννης καὶ Ἰαμβρῆς ἀντέστησαν Μωϋσεῖ, οὕτως καὶ οὗτοι ἀνθίστανται τῇ ἀληθείᾳ, ἄνθρωποι κατεφθαρμένοι τὸν νοῦν, ἀδόκιμοι περὶ τὴν πίστιν.
verses.mistral: These men are opposing the truth, just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses. Such are the corrupt in mind and rejected concerning faith.
verses.kjv: Now as Jannes and Jambres withstood Moses, so do these also resist the truth: men of corrupt minds, reprobate concerning the faith.

verses.greek: ἀλλ’ οὐ προκόψουσιν ἐπὶ πλεῖον, ἡ γὰρ ἄνοια αὐτῶν ἔκδηλος ἔσται πᾶσιν, ὡς καὶ ἡ ἐκείνων ἐγένετο.
verses.mistral: These men will go no further; for their foolishness will be evident to all, just as was the case with those in former times.
verses.kjv: But they shall proceed no further: for their folly shall be manifest unto all men, as their's also was.

verses.greek: Σὺ δὲ ⸀παρηκολούθησάς μου τῇ διδασκαλίᾳ, τῇ ἀγωγῇ, τῇ προθέσει, τῇ πίστει, τῇ μακροθυμίᾳ, τῇ ἀγάπῃ, τῇ ὑπομονῇ,
verses.mistral: Followed me in my teaching, the guidance, the persecutions, the faith, the longsuffering, the love, the endurance, This is the English translation of 2 Timothy 3:10 (Koine Greek), with no additional context or notes. The previous verse for context provided was 2 Timothy 3:9, which helps to understand that Paul is speaking about people who have followed him in his teachings and way of life, including persecutions he faced.
verses.kjv: But thou hast fully known my doctrine, manner of life, purpose, faith, longsuffering, charity, patience,

verses.greek: τοῖς διωγμοῖς, τοῖς παθήμασιν, οἷά μοι ἐγένετο ἐν Ἀντιοχείᾳ, ἐν Ἰκονίῳ, ἐν Λύστροις, οἵους διωγμοὺς ὑπήνεγκα· καὶ ἐκ πάντων με ἐρρύσατο ὁ κύριος.
verses.mistral: Persecutions, sufferings, such as happened to me in Antioch, Iconium, Lystra, these persecutions I endured; and the Lord delivered me from all of them.
verses.kjv: Persecutions, afflictions, which came unto me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra; what persecutions I endured: but out of them all the Lord delivered me.

verses.greek: καὶ πάντες δὲ οἱ θέλοντες ⸂ζῆν εὐσεβῶς⸃ ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ διωχθήσονται·
verses.mistral: And all who desire to live uprightly in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.
verses.kjv: Yea, and all that will live godly in Christ Jesus shall suffer persecution.

verses.greek: πονηροὶ δὲ ἄνθρωποι καὶ γόητες προκόψουσιν ἐπὶ τὸ χεῖρον, πλανῶντες καὶ πλανώμενοι.
verses.mistral: Evil men and impostors will grow worse and worse, deceiving and being deceived.
verses.kjv: But evil men and seducers shall wax worse and worse, deceiving, and being deceived.

verses.greek: σὺ δὲ μένε ἐν οἷς ἔμαθες καὶ ἐπιστώθης, εἰδὼς παρὰ ⸀τίνων ἔμαθες,
verses.mistral: Remain in what you have learned and received, knowing from whom you learned it.
verses.kjv: But continue thou in the things which thou hast learned and hast been assured of, knowing of whom thou hast learned them;

verses.greek: καὶ ὅτι ἀπὸ βρέφους ⸀ἱερὰ γράμματα οἶδας, τὰ δυνάμενά σε σοφίσαι εἰς σωτηρίαν διὰ πίστεως τῆς ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ·
verses.mistral: And that from childhood you have known the holy writings, which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
verses.kjv: And that from a child thou hast known the holy scriptures, which are able to make thee wise unto salvation through faith which is in Christ Jesus.

verses.greek: πᾶσα γραφὴ θεόπνευστος καὶ ὠφέλιμος πρὸς διδασκαλίαν, πρὸς ⸀ἐλεγμόν, πρὸς ἐπανόρθωσιν, πρὸς παιδείαν τὴν ἐν δικαιοσύνῃ,
verses.mistral: All scripture is inspired by God and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, for training in righteousness.
verses.kjv: All scripture is given by inspiration of God, and is profitable for doctrine, for reproof, for correction, for instruction in righteousness:

verses.greek: ἵνα ἄρτιος ᾖ ὁ τοῦ θεοῦ ἄνθρωπος, πρὸς πᾶν ἔργον ἀγαθὸν ἐξηρτισμένος.
verses.mistral: Adept is the man of God, prepared for every good work.
verses.kjv: That the man of God may be perfect, throughly furnished unto all good works.

verses.greek: ⸀Διαμαρτύρομαι ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ ⸂Χριστοῦ Ἰησοῦ⸃, τοῦ μέλλοντος κρίνειν ζῶντας καὶ νεκρούς, ⸀καὶ τὴν ἐπιφάνειαν αὐτοῦ καὶ τὴν βασιλείαν αὐτοῦ·
verses.mistral: I am bearing witness before God and Christ Jesus, who is to judge the living and the dead, and their eternal kingdom.
verses.kjv: I charge thee therefore before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, who shall judge the quick and the dead at his appearing and his kingdom;

verses.greek: κήρυξον τὸν λόγον, ἐπίστηθι εὐκαίρως ἀκαίρως, ἔλεγξον, ἐπιτίμησον, παρακάλεσον, ἐν πάσῃ μακροθυμίᾳ καὶ διδαχῇ.
verses.mistral: Preach the word, be instant in season, out of season; reprove, rebuke, exhort with all longsuffering and doctrine.
verses.kjv: TIMOTHY

verses.greek: ἔσται γὰρ καιρὸς ὅτε τῆς ὑγιαινούσης διδασκαλίας οὐκ ἀνέξονται, ἀλλὰ κατὰ τὰς ⸂ἰδίας ἐπιθυμίας⸃ ἑαυτοῖς ἐπισωρεύσουσιν διδασκάλους κνηθόμενοι τὴν ἀκοήν,
verses.mistral: There will be a time when they will not endure sound doctrine, but according to their own desires they will gather teachers for themselves, tuning their ears.
verses.kjv: For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine; but after their own lusts shall they heap to themselves teachers, having itching ears;

verses.greek: καὶ ἀπὸ μὲν τῆς ἀληθείας τὴν ἀκοὴν ἀποστρέψουσιν, ἐπὶ δὲ τοὺς μύθους ἐκτραπήσονται.
verses.mistral: Turn away from the truth and turn aside to myths.
verses.kjv: And they shall turn away their ears from the truth, and shall be turned unto fables.

verses.greek: σὺ δὲ νῆφε ἐν πᾶσιν, κακοπάθησον, ἔργον ποίησον εὐαγγελιστοῦ, τὴν διακονίαν σου πληροφόρησον.
verses.mistral: You must persevere in all things, suffer hardships, do the work of an evangelist, fulfill your ministry.
verses.kjv: But watch thou in all things, endure afflictions, do the work of an evangelist, make full proof of thy ministry.

verses.greek: Ἐγὼ γὰρ ἤδη σπένδομαι, καὶ ὁ καιρὸς τῆς ⸂ἀναλύσεώς μου⸃ ἐφέστηκεν.
verses.mistral: My release is at hand, and the time of my departure has arrived.
verses.kjv: For I am now ready to be offered, and the time of my departure is at hand.

verses.greek: τὸν ⸂καλὸν ἀγῶνα⸃ ἠγώνισμαι, τὸν δρόμον τετέλεκα, τὴν πίστιν τετήρηκα·
verses.mistral: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith
verses.kjv: I have fought a good fight, I have finished my course, I have kept the faith:

verses.greek: λοιπὸν ἀπόκειταί μοι ὁ τῆς δικαιοσύνης στέφανος, ὃν ἀποδώσει μοι ὁ κύριος ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ, ὁ δίκαιος κριτής, οὐ μόνον δὲ ἐμοὶ ἀλλὰ καὶ πᾶσιν τοῖς ἠγαπηκόσι τὴν ἐπιφάνειαν αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: The following crown of righteousness is laid up for me, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will award to me on that day; not only to me, but also to all who have loved His appearing.
verses.kjv: Henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, shall give me at that day: and not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing.

verses.greek: Σπούδασον ἐλθεῖν πρός με ταχέως·
verses.mistral: Hurry to come to me quickly
verses.kjv: Do thy diligence to come shortly unto me:

verses.greek: Δημᾶς γάρ με ⸀ἐγκατέλιπεν ἀγαπήσας τὸν νῦν αἰῶνα, καὶ ἐπορεύθη εἰς Θεσσαλονίκην, Κρήσκης εἰς Γαλατίαν, Τίτος εἰς Δαλματίαν·
verses.mistral: Abandoned me is Demas, loving this present world, and went to Thessalonica; Crispus to Galatia, Titus to Dalmatia.
verses.kjv: For Demas hath forsaken me, having loved this present world, and is departed unto Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia, Titus unto Dalmatia.

verses.greek: Λουκᾶς ἐστιν μόνος μετ’ ἐμοῦ. Μᾶρκον ἀναλαβὼν ἄγε μετὰ σεαυτοῦ, ἔστιν γάρ μοι εὔχρηστος εἰς διακονίαν,
verses.mistral: Luke is alone with me. Mark, receiving him, bring him with you, for he is profitable to me for service.
verses.kjv: Only Luke is with me. Take Mark, and bring him with thee: for he is profitable to me for the ministry.

verses.greek: Τυχικὸν δὲ ἀπέστειλα εἰς Ἔφεσον.
verses.mistral: Sent to Ephesus was he, indeed.
verses.kjv: And Tychicus have I sent to Ephesus.

verses.greek: τὸν φαιλόνην, ὃν ⸀ἀπέλιπον ἐν Τρῳάδι παρὰ Κάρπῳ, ἐρχόμενος φέρε, καὶ τὰ βιβλία, μάλιστα τὰς μεμβράνας.
verses.mistral: Faithful is the one left in Troas, whom I left behind with Carpus, when he comes, welcome him to my room, and the books especially the parchments.
verses.kjv: The cloke that I left at Troas with Carpus, when thou comest, bring with thee, and the books, but especially the parchments.

verses.greek: Ἀλέξανδρος ὁ χαλκεὺς πολλά μοι κακὰ ἐνεδείξατο— ⸀ἀποδώσει αὐτῷ ὁ κύριος κατὰ τὰ ἔργα αὐτοῦ—
verses.mistral: Alexander the copperworker has shown me many evil things—the Lord will repay him according to his works.
verses.kjv: Alexander the coppersmith did me much evil: the Lord reward him according to his works:

verses.greek: ὃν καὶ σὺ φυλάσσου, λίαν γὰρ ⸀ἀντέστη τοῖς ἡμετέροις λόγοις.
verses.mistral: You must carefully observe that one, for he is subversive to our messages.
verses.kjv: Of whom be thou ware also; for he hath greatly withstood our words.

verses.greek: Ἐν τῇ πρώτῃ μου ἀπολογίᾳ οὐδείς μοι ⸀παρεγένετο, ἀλλὰ πάντες με ⸀ἐγκατέλιπον— μὴ αὐτοῖς λογισθείη—
verses.mistral: Abandoned me in my first defense no one did, but all forsook me.
verses.kjv: At my first answer no man stood with me, but all men forsook me: I pray God that it may not be laid to their charge.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ κύριός μοι παρέστη καὶ ἐνεδυνάμωσέν με, ἵνα δι’ ἐμοῦ τὸ κήρυγμα πληροφορηθῇ καὶ ⸀ἀκούσωσιν πάντα τὰ ἔθνη, καὶ ἐρρύσθην ἐκ στόματος λέοντος.
verses.mistral: The Lord stood by me and strengthened me, that through me the message might be fully proclaimed and heard among all the Gentiles, and I was delivered from the lion's mouth.
verses.kjv: Notwithstanding the Lord stood with me, and strengthened me; that by me the preaching might be fully known, and that all the Gentiles might hear: and I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.

verses.greek: ⸀ῥύσεταί με ὁ κύριος ἀπὸ παντὸς ἔργου πονηροῦ καὶ σώσει εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν αὐτοῦ τὴν ἐπουράνιον· ᾧ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων, ἀμήν.
verses.mistral: The Lord shall deliver me from every evil work and save me for His heavenly kingdom, to whom be glory forevermore, amen.
verses.kjv: And the Lord shall deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve me unto his heavenly kingdom: to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.

verses.greek: Ἄσπασαι Πρίσκαν καὶ Ἀκύλαν καὶ τὸν Ὀνησιφόρου οἶκον.
verses.mistral: Greet Priscilla and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.
verses.kjv: Salute Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.

verses.greek: Ἔραστος ἔμεινεν ἐν Κορίνθῳ, Τρόφιμον δὲ ⸀ἀπέλιπον ἐν Μιλήτῳ ἀσθενοῦντα.
verses.mistral: Erastus remained in Corinth, Trophimus left sick at Miletus.
verses.kjv: Erastus abode at Corinth: but Trophimus have I left at Miletum sick.

verses.greek: Σπούδασον πρὸ χειμῶνος ἐλθεῖν. Ἀσπάζεταί σε Εὔβουλος καὶ Πούδης καὶ Λίνος καὶ Κλαυδία καὶ οἱ ἀδελφοὶ πάντες.
verses.mistral: Prepare for the winter's coming. Euboulos, Pudens, Linus, Claudia, and all the brothers send greetings to you.
verses.kjv: Do thy diligence to come before winter. Eubulus greeteth thee, and Pudens, and Linus, and Claudia, and all the brethren.

verses.greek: Ὁ ⸀κύριος μετὰ τοῦ πνεύματός σου. ἡ χάρις μεθ’ ⸀ὑμῶν.
verses.mistral: The Lord is with your spirit. Grace be with you.
verses.kjv: The Lord Jesus Christ be with thy spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.
